prompts: The immune system protects the body against pathogens through various mechanisms. White blood cells, such as macrophages and lymphocytes, play a crucial role by identifying and eliminating pathogens. Antibodies, produced by B cells, specifically recognize and bind to foreign antigens on the surface of pathogens, neutralizing them or marking them for destruction by other immune cells. T cells assist by killing infected cells or helping activate other immune cells. This coordinated response helps the body effectively defend against infections and disease.
